summary: Improved matching and readability for several common fighter item-throw routines by replacing many M2C_FIELD/raw-offset accesses with typed Fighter/ftCo_Fighter fields, GET_FIGHTER, named callbacks, input fields, item state fields, and motion-variable fields. The PR also corrected ftCommonData layout around offset 0x3FC, allowing code to use p_ftCommonData->x3FC directly, and removed an obsolete global label symbol at ftCo_800959A0 after the function boundary/match improved.
what_changed: Reworked ftCo_80095328 from a large m2c-style implementation using HSD_GObj*, void*, M2C_FIELD, gotos, and temporary variables into typed logic using ftCo_GObj*, Fighter*, GET_FIGHTER, fp->item_gobj, fp->input.cstick/lstick, fp->x673/x674, fp->facing_dir, fp->x2224_b1, and bool returns.

Simplified item-throw motion selection in ftCo_80095328 by computing an angle from C-stick or L-stick input, checking common-data thresholds with fabs_inline, and assigning msid through compact ternary expressions instead of many raw var_r0 branches.
Changed ftCo_80095700 and ftCo_800957F4 to use GET_FIGHTER(gobj) instead of direct gobj->user_data access.

Reworked ftCo_800958FC to initialize cmd_vars and throw_flags with typed Fighter fields, reuse getAnimSpeed(gobj, msid), write mv.co.itemthrow.facing_dir, set named callbacks/accessors, and use x2222_b0 instead of raw bit extraction.

Cleaned ftCo_80095A30 by replacing raw offsets for L-stick input, facing direction, jump startup, item_gobj, and stick-age counters with typed fields such as fp->input.lstick, fp->facing_dir, fp->co_attrs.jump_startup_time, fp->x673, and fp->x674.
Adjusted ftCo_80095EFC stack/local structure by hoisting Vec3 locals, adding PAD_STACK(0x8), and replacing component-wise Vec3 assignment with direct struct assignment.
Updated struct ftCommonData in src/melee/ft/types.h so offset 0x3FC is represented as int x3FC, shifting x400/x404/x408 to their correct named offsets.
Removed ftCo_800959A0 from config/GALE01/symbols.txt as a global label, consistent with improved matching around ftCo_800958FC/ftCo_80095A30.
smart_moves: Introduced the missing ftCommonData field at 0x3FC as an int, which made repeated raw M2C_FIELD(p_ftCommonData, int*, 0x3FC) comparisons expressible as p_ftCommonData->x3FC.
Used GET_FIGHTER consistently to reduce direct user_data casts and align item-throw code with existing fighter decomp style.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack shape with explicit PAD_STACK calls while still simplifying the source.
Factored animation speed calculation through the existing getAnimSpeed inline rather than duplicating the p_ftCommonData->x400 and it_8026B334 logic.
Converted raw callback-slot writes to named Fighter callback fields, making the control-flow intent clearer without changing behavior.
Kept motion IDs numeric where no stronger enum names were provided, avoiding speculative naming while improving surrounding logic.
Used direct Vec3 assignment for fp->mv.co.itemthrow4.x8 = vec0 where the struct type made it safe and clearer.
decomp_lessons: When repeated M2C_FIELD accesses target common-data offsets, check whether the shared data struct is missing a field; adding one typed member can unlock cleaner matches across a function.
Typed Fighter input fields can collapse large m2c temporaries and gotos into ordinary control flow while retaining the same threshold logic.
For fighter code, prefer GET_FIGHTER(gobj) over direct gobj->user_data casts when the surrounding project style supports it.
Bitfield names such as x2222_b0 and x2224_b1 are valuable matching/readability aids compared with manual byte shifts and OR masks.
Callback fields should be named in the Fighter struct and used directly when offsets are known; this makes lifecycle setup in motion-state transitions much easier to audit.
Small layout fixes in headers can remove global labels or split points from symbols.txt by improving compiler output and function boundary matching.
Do not over-name motion IDs unless the PR provides evidence; this change retained numeric msid values while clarifying the decision tree.
naming_conventions: Use ftCo_GObj* for common-fighter GObj parameters when the function is specifically operating on common fighter objects.
Use Fighter* or ftCo_Fighter* fp for the typed fighter data obtained from GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Use descriptive locals such as angle and msid in place of m2c temporaries like var_f1 and var_r4 when the role is clear.
Use p_ftCommonData->x3FC after adding the corresponding struct member instead of raw offset access.
Use named fields fp->cmd_vars, fp->throw_flags, fp->mv.co.itemthrow.facing_dir, fp->accessory4_cb, fp->take_dmg_cb, fp->phys_cb, and fp->coll_cb instead of offset-based M2C_FIELD writes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Retained PAD_STACK(0x8) or equivalent padding in functions where stack layout still mattered for matching.
Converted explicit absolute-value branches to fabs_inline threshold checks in ftCo_80095328.
Reused the getAnimSpeed inline in ftCo_800958FC to match the shared computation used by ftCo_800957F4.
Moved Vec3 local declarations and preserved stack padding in ftCo_80095EFC, indicating stack/local ordering was still matching-sensitive.
Replaced component-wise Vec3 writes with a direct struct assignment where the compiler could still match.
Removed an intermediate global label symbol once the surrounding C matched well enough not to require ftCo_800959A0 as a separate label.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_80095328 or the numeric item-throw motion IDs 0x5E through 0x75.
Search for other uses of M2C_FIELD(p_ftCommonData, int*, 0x3FC) that can be converted to p_ftCommonData->x3FC.
Check whether ftCo_80094EA4 shares enough code with ftCo_80095328 to benefit from the same typed-field cleanup, as the todo notes shared code.
Look for additional Fighter callback offset writes around 0x21A4, 0x21A8, 0x21BC, and 0x21DC that can use named fields.
Search for remaining item-throw code using raw item_gobj offset 0x1974 or input offsets 0x620/0x624/0x638/0x63C.
